---
title: "Evaluation"
pagetitle: "Evaluation — EM 619 MLPA"
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Tentative
This grading scheme is **tentative** and may be modified during the semester based on student feedback. Any changes will be discussed in class and adopted by consensus between the instructors and students before implementation.
:::

## Grade Distribution

The final grade is computed from the total score using the following weights:

| Component | Weight |
|:----------|:------:|
| **Assignments** (in groups of 2, followed by viva) | **45%** |
| **Examination I** (Mid-semester) | **20%** |
| **Examination II** (End-semester) | **25%** |
| **Attendance & Class Participation** | **10%** |

## Assignments & Tutorials

::: {.callout-note}
- There will be about **3–5 assignments** through the semester.
- Questions are based on **applying course concepts** to arrive at solutions; most assignments require **computer programming** in Python.
- Assignments are done in **groups of 2** and evaluated via a **viva voce**.
- You are encouraged to discuss problems with peers, TAs, and instructors **before** submission.
:::

## Quizzes

::: {.callout-note}
- About **4–5 announced quizzes**, taken at the beginning, middle, or end of a lecture.
- Typically **conceptual questions** requiring short answers — one-line, multiple-choice, or fill-in-the-blank.
- Questions may come from content **already covered** or from material to be **covered in the next few classes**.
- Each quiz carries **equal weight**, irrespective of its maximum marks.
:::

## Examinations

Two examinations are held during the semester:

- **Examination I** — mid-semester (20%)
- **Examination II** — end-semester (25%)

## Attendance & Class Participation

::: {.callout-note}
Students are expected to **ask questions and participate** in discussions. Those who typically remain passive will be awarded zero participation marks.

Attendance marks are computed from the ratio of **classes attended** to **total lectures**.
:::
